meta.note: Kuwait + Saudi Arabia + Turkey sweep (published books). Deduped against existing KW/SA/TR records and the wider DB; many famous titles already existed and get a country ban-add via existing_book_id. Held (low confidence): KW 'Why We Write About Ourselves', SA 'al-Aghlal'. Lost in Mecca and 'Maps of Wandering' are the same al-Essa novel (one entry). The DB has a pre-existing 1984 / Nineteen Eighty-Four duplicate; the KW ban is attached to the canonical id 4. Sources: ArabLit, Al-Fanar, Hyperallergic, Document Journal, The Markaz Review, PEN, The National, Bianet, EurasiaNet, Wikipedia(+cited).

entries.title: Mama Hissa's Mice
entries.description_ban: Dystopian novel of sectarian strife in Kuwait; banned by the Ministry of Information for 'advocating sectarianism', later overturned after the author sued.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban (later overturned); documented by ArabLit.

entries.title: Lost in Mecca
entries.description_ban: Novel (also rendered 'Maps of Wandering') about a Kuwaiti child kidnapped during Hajj; passages describing rape were deemed sexually provocative and the book was banned.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban; documented by ArabLit.

entries.title: The Brothers Karamazov
entries.description_ban: Among roughly 1,000 titles barred from the 2018 Kuwait International Book Fair during the Ministry of Information censorship wave.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair ban; documented in press coverage of the Kuwait ban wave.

entries.title: Sophie's World
entries.description_ban: Norwegian philosophy novel banned in Kuwait during the 2014–2018 Ministry of Information ban wave.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by ArabLit.

entries.title: The Little Mermaid
entries.description_ban: Disney children's picture-book adaptation banned because the mermaid's top was deemed too revealing — the case that drew international attention to Kuwait's book censorship.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Iconic title-specific Kuwait ban; documented by Document Journal.

entries.title: The Scent of the Tango
entries.description_ban: Kuwaiti novel banned by the Ministry of Information; the author challenged the decision in court and won.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ministry ban with a court challenge; documented by ArabLit.

entries.title: The Hunchback of Notre-Dame
entries.description_ban: The 19th-century French classic was among the ~4,390 titles barred by Kuwait's Ministry of Information censorship committee (2014–2018). Adds a Kuwait ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Kuwait ban; documented by Hyperallergic.

entries.title: The Divine Comedy
entries.description_ban: Dante's medieval epic was banned by Kuwait's Ministry of Information during the 2014–2018 ban wave. Adds a Kuwait ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Kuwait ban; documented by ArabLit.

entries.title: Zorba the Greek
entries.description_ban: Banned and re-approved by Kuwait's Ministry several times within one year, illustrating arbitrary censorship. Adds a Kuwait restriction to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Kuwait restriction; documented by ArabLit.

entries.title: The Forty Rules of Love
entries.description_ban: The Arabic translation was banned in Kuwait while the English edition remained permitted — a documented language-dependent restriction. Adds a Kuwait restriction to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Kuwait restriction; documented by Al-Fanar Media.

entries.description_ban: One publisher's Kuwaiti edition of Orwell's novel was banned while another publisher's edition of the same title was allowed. Adds a Kuwait restriction to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Kuwait restriction; documented by Al-Fanar Media.

entries.title: I Know Why the Caged Bird Sings
entries.description_ban: Angelou's memoir was among the titles barred by Kuwait's Ministry of Information. Adds a Kuwait ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Kuwait ban; documented by Hyperallergic.

entries.title: Throwing Sparks
entries.description_ban: IPAF-winning novel on corruption and moral decay in Jeddah; banned in Saudi Arabia and withdrawn from the Riyadh Book Fair.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by The Markaz Review.

entries.title: The Dove's Necklace
entries.description_ban: Joint 2011 IPAF winner depicting a crime-ridden, corrupt Mecca; banned in the author's home country.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia with citation.

entries.title: Wolves of the Crescent Moon
entries.description_ban: Novel of three Riyadh outsiders; banned in Saudi Arabia, forcing publication in Beirut.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia with citation.

entries.title: Shumaisi
entries.description_ban: Second volume of al-Hamad's 'Phantoms of the Deserted Alley' trilogy; banned in Saudi Arabia for its treatment of sexuality, politics and religion.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia with citation.

entries.title: al-Karadib
entries.description_ban: Third volume of al-Hamad's trilogy; angered clerics and drew fatwas, banned in Saudi Arabia.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia with citation.

entries.title: Hend and the Soldiers
entries.description_ban: Novella denouncing the religious police and demanding greater freedom for women; banned in the author's native Saudi Arabia.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by ArabLit.

entries.title: Instructions Within
entries.description_ban: Poetry collection banned from distribution; cited as evidence of 'atheism' in the apostasy case that led to Fayadh's imprisonment.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by PEN America.

entries.title: Revolution 2.0
entries.description_ban: Egyptian-uprising memoir; copies confiscated at the 2014 Riyadh International Book Fair.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair confiscation; documented by The National.

entries.title: When Will the Saudi Woman Drive a Car?
entries.description_ban: Book on women's right to drive; confiscated at the 2014 Riyadh Book Fair.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific book-fair confiscation; documented by The National.

entries.title: The Imam's Army
entries.description_ban: Manuscript on the Gülen movement seized by police before publication and banned by court as an 'illegal organizational document'; later released online and in print in defiance.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific pre-publication seizure/ban; documented by PEN America.

entries.title: Dream Divided
entries.description_ban: An Istanbul court ruled the novel 'terrorist propaganda' under Anti-Terror Law No. 3713; banned and copies confiscated. Charges were later dropped but the publication/sale ban remains.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by PEN International and Duvar.

entries.title: Veda
entries.description_ban: Novel touching on the 1937–38 Dersim events; banned from publication, distribution and sale and confiscated in January 2023.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by PEN International.

entries.title: Çığlık
entries.description_ban: Companion Dersim-themed novel ('Scream'); banned from publication, distribution and sale and confiscated alongside Veda in January 2023.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented by PEN International.

entries.title: The Soft Machine
entries.description_ban: The Turkish edition (Sel Publishing) was prosecuted for obscenity after a state board called it 'injurious to sexuality'; tried in Istanbul, case deferred in 2012.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific obscenity prosecution; documented by EurasiaNet and Bianet.

entries.title: The Forty Days of Musa Dagh
entries.description_ban: Novel on Armenian resistance during the genocide; banned in Turkey shortly after publication, and Turkey pressured MGM to halt a film adaptation.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia and NAASR.
entries.source_name: Wikipedia (The Forty Days of Musa Dagh)

entries.title: The Order of Eastern Anatolia
entries.description_ban: Sociological study of Kurdish eastern Turkey ('Doğu Anadolu'nun Düzeni') that made Beşikçi a target; banned and the basis of his prosecution. Distinct from his Kurdistan title already held.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia and academic sources.

entries.title: Temple Prostitutes
entries.description_ban: Crime novel (Başkomser Nevzat: Tapınak Fahişeleri) ruled 'harmful to minors' by the State Board for the Protection of Minors; must be sold in sealed envelopes off open shelves with a +18 stamp.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific state restriction (sealed-envelope); documented by PEN International and Duvar.

entries.title: Sexing the Cherry
entries.description_ban: Deemed 'obscene' by Turkey's State Board for the Protection of Minors; ordered sold in sealed envelopes with a minors warning.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific state restriction (sealed-envelope); documented by PEN International and Duvar.

entries.title: Snuff
entries.description_ban: The Turkish edition (Ayrıntı Publishing) was prosecuted for 'obscene material' under Article 226; case deferred in 2012, no conviction. Adds a Turkey case to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Turkey obscenity prosecution; documented by PEN International and Bianet.

entries.title: The Satanic Verses
entries.description_ban: Turkey prohibited importing the book and any Turkish translation; it remains unpublished in Turkish. Aziz Nesin's serialized excerpts triggered the 1993 Sivas massacre. Adds a Turkey ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Turkey ban; documented via Wikipedia and Bianet.

entries.title: The Grapes of Wrath
entries.description_ban: Eleven publishers and eight booksellers were tried before an Istanbul martial-law tribunal in February 1973 for 'spreading propaganda unfavourable to the state'; copies subject to confiscation. Adds a Turkey ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Turkey ban; documented via Index on Censorship (cited).
entries.source_name: Great Falls College MSU (citing Index on Censorship)

entries.title: Tropic of Capricorn
entries.description_ban: The 1985 Turkish translation was prosecuted for obscenity; translator and publisher were convicted and fined c.1986, prompting ~40 publishers to jointly reissue it in protest. Adds a Turkey case to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Turkey obscenity prosecution; documented in academic literature.
